body.title: The organizing-question cascade — every level has its organizing questions; intention descends by filtering through them; conflict has no surface
body.text:
# D-organizing-question-cascade-2026-07-07
**title:** The organizing-question cascade
**date:** 2026-07-07
**status:** open
**spawned_tickets:** T-organizing-questions-per-level

## Decision narrative
Every level of the build stack — at every scale: design, architecture, ticket, workflow, file, symbol — has a finite SET OF ORGANIZING QUESTIONS: the few structural questions that shape any decision at that level. That set IS the level's rubric (score/adjudicate by how well a candidate answers the level's organizing questions) AND the checklist its resolution loop runs — same object.

Intention enters at the top and DESCENDS: at each level it passes through that level's organizing questions, which filter it into the substance handed to the next level down. Code at the bottom is not authored — it is what remains once the intention has been filtered through every level's questions. Code is the INSTANTIATION of the answered questions.

CONFLICT-ELIMINATION (the load-bearing insight, from Akien's relationship-coaching frame): goals/intentions don't conflict — STRATEGIES for meeting them conflict. Conflict needs a degree of freedom to form in — an unpinned place where a level got to choose a strategy the level above didn't determine. If each level is the deterministic filtered descent of the level above (the answer to its organizing questions, not a free strategic choice), there is no unpinned freedom in the stack and conflict has no surface to form on. You don't resolve conflicts; you remove the room they'd form in. This is BETTER THAN REQUIREMENTS: requirements are a flat list that can pull against each other; organizing questions are hierarchical/generative — each level only answers within what the level above already pinned, so there is no lateral room for two answers to fight.

This GENERALIZES proof-on-close up the whole stack: a level without a named rubric (organizing-question set) is exactly where cognition can't yet compile. Levels that already compile have an implicit set (code -> does-it-pass; model-select -> cost/capability argmin; build -> the evaluator judge panel). The genuinely-empty rows are DESIGN and ARCHITECTURE.

## Resolution loop (the runtime at each level)
Self-similar, fractal at every level. For each organizing question at a level, resolve — four outcomes:
1. Answered by an EXTANT PATTERN -> compile, descend (cheap/common path).
2. Answerable but answer implies MISSING WORK -> flag/spawn it (no test -> flag creating the test; not checked against design patterns -> flag the check).
3. NOVEL, no extant pattern -> escalate to the meta-question ('what are the questions to determine the questions here?') -> the expensive resolver (LLM/human). The only place the LLM is needed — the compile-away-the-routine thesis.
4. Can't resolve now -> spawn a WATCH-FOR (learn-more pattern): park, accumulate signal, resolve later.
The stage LOOPS until every organizing question is resolved (pattern / spawned-work / escalation-answer / watch-for), then descends.

## Grounding (code inventory 2026-07-07)
- Adjudicator machinery EXISTS: unseen_university/devices/evaluator/device.py — 3-voter judge panel, majority verdict.
- Candidate generation THIN: MAX_CANDIDATES=1; no N-solution tournament. But note: if organizing questions fully pin the descent, competition largely COLLAPSES (one filtered answer falls out) — the judge panel is the RESIDUAL for underdetermined/novel nodes, not the main path.
- Missing substrate for outcome-1: devlab/runtime/memory/design_patterns/ is EMPTY. A repo-pattern-collection ticket already exists (per Akien) to populate it — that is the extant-pattern library the loop queries.
- The resolution-loop CONDUCTOR is already ticketed: T-substrate-experiment-cascade ('the conductor that walks levels before escalating to LLM'), T-experiment-primitive (EPIC, Igor's strategy-2 substrate), T-inference-outcome-learner (observe->discover). This decision UNIFIES these under one principle rather than starting from zero.
- Proof/prereg corpus (proof_store.py, inference/prereg.py) is the likely ground-truth substrate for an eventual learned/evolving rubric.

## Cortical-columns grounding
The cascade IS predictive-coding cortex. Mountcastle canonical microcircuit repeated at every level = the self-similar loop. A column's organizing questions = the questions it PREDICTS WITH. Extant-pattern answer = low-surprise learned prediction (compiled). Novelty/escalation = prediction error propagating up. Watch-For = holding a prediction open under uncertainty (active inference). Conflict-free descent = predictions cohere downward, error only where underdetermined. The build stack and the neocortex are the same object: hierarchical question-driven prediction machines. Ties project_cortical_columns_thesis + project_graph_trees_self_aware_telos + project_compiled_inference_vision into one frame.

## Scope / timing
This is a refactoring of EVERYTHING — north-star tier. GATED: to be built only AFTER current debt-clearing (aider-port batch + repo cleanup). Filed durably now so it survives compaction; NOT for immediate sprint. Full per-level ticket decomposition + audit chain deferred until the gate opens and the vision has settled — auditing a still-crystallizing north-star only freezes a smaller version of it.

## Hypothesis
A finite set of organizing questions can be named for each build level; naming a level's set makes that level's decision derivable-by-descent rather than freely-chosen, which removes the degrees of freedom where conflict forms. Falsifiable first slice (design level): a named finite organizing-question set exists and a reviewer can adjudicate two competing designs — including a near-tie — using only that set; if it can't discriminate the near-tie, the missing organizing question has been found.

## Measurement Signal
The organizing-question set(s) exist in the memory store and pass audit-design (decidable criteria, named ground-truth source); a reviewer adjudicates a hand-picked pair AND a near-tie pair using only the set — vs today, where design_patterns/ and judge/ store dirs are empty.

## Intention
I intend that every level of the cognition-compiling pipeline has an explicit organizing-question set, so each level's decision resolves by deterministic descent (only the genuinely-novel escalating to the expensive resolver) and conflict has no unpinned surface to form on.
